summary: Everything on this page comes directly from the company's own annual benefits filing — the Form 5500 that nearly every U.S. employer with a benefits plan must file with the Department of Labor, together with its Schedule A insurance attachments. Nothing here is estimated: the participant count, covered lives, carriers, and broker commissions are shown as filed.
For this demonstration the company and carrier names are anonymized, but the figures are real. Filings publish on a lag, so this snapshot reflects the 2024 plan year — the most recent available.

moves.headline: Find the filing
moves.detail: Filtered the DOL all-filers dataset to this employer's EIN + plan number and took the most recent plan year (2024-01-01 → 2024-12-31).

moves.headline: Report it as filed
moves.detail: Participants (active employees) from the main form; covered lives, carriers, and per-carrier broker commissions from Schedule A; benefit codes decoded per the DOL line-8b instructions. Nothing modeled or imputed — absent items (e.g. Schedule C) are flagged, not filled.

moves.headline: Participants vs. covered lives
moves.detail: Participants counts EMPLOYEES; covered lives counts everyone enrolled, including spouses and children (Schedule A enrollment). Both appear on this page and they answer different questions.

limitations: Annual filing — figures are roughly a year old by the time they publish.
The broker of record is disclosed on Schedule C, which is not attached to this filing; the broker name shown is illustrative. Commissions are as filed.
Filing fields are self-reported by the plan sponsor.
Anonymization changes names only — never counts or dollars.
